arts.ttl: Napoli & Juventus Set Sights on Verona Rising Star
arts.sum: Hellas Verona wingback Jackson Tchatchoua has reportedly drawn inquiries from several top clubs, including Napoli and Juventus. According to Calciomercato, the two Serie A heavyweights have dispatched scouts to monitor the 22-year-old's progress at Stadio Marc'Antonio Bentegodi. The post Napoli & Juventus Set Sights on Verona Rising Star appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Napoli & Lukaku Eye Victory as Belgian Striker Returns to Cagliari
arts.sum: Romelu Lukaku looks set for his first Serie A start for Napoli on Sunday evening as Antonio Conte's side meet Cagliari at the Unipol Domus. A trip to Sardinia stirs mixed feelings for the Belgian striker, who, despite his impressive numbers against the Islanders, doesn't have fond memories of this venue. The post Napoli & Lukaku Eye Victory as Belgian Striker Returns to Cagliari appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Napoli and Juventus battle for Verona’s rising star Tchatchoua
arts.sum: Verona’s sporting director Sean Sogliano has once again demonstrated his talent for unearthing hidden gems in the transfer market. His latest discovery, Jackson Tchatchoua, was acquired from Charleroi for a mere €2 million and has quickly become a valuable asset for the club. Calciomercato report on how Tchatchoua’s rapid rise to prominence has caught the attention of several top clubs, with Napoli showing strong interest in the young right-back. The Partenopei are reportedly ready to make a move for the Cameroonian as early as January, seeing him as a potential reinforcement for their squad. arts.ttl: Chelsea return for Victor Osimhen in January
arts.sum: Osimhen joined Galatasaray in a shock loan from Napoli after a deadline day transfer to Chelsea (and to Saudi Arabia) failed to be completed. Chelsea could return for Victor Osimhen in January despite his season-long loan deal at Galatasaray.

Cagliari manager Davide Nicola expressed his appreciation for the compliments he received from Napoli’s coach Antonio Conte during a pre-match press conference ahead of the Cagliari-Napoli fixture. Nicola’s response showcased mutual respect between the two managers and his focus on continuous improvement. Nicola stated, “Conte’s compliments are pleasing. They are the same that I, from a different position, extend to him as he is a top-level coach. I’m glad that he appreciates me. I don’t pay much attention to what is said about me; rather, I try to always learn new things.” Reflecting on Cagliari’s recent performance against Lecce, Nicola The post Davide Nicola: ‘Conte’s compliments are pleasing’ appeared first on FootItalia.com.

Chelsea transfer news: Victor Osimhen January hope as 'secret clause' revealed
arts.sum: Chelsea are reportedly given hope of signing Napoli striker Victor Osimhen in January due to a break clause in his contract with loan club Galatasaray.
